title: Limonium callianthum
uses: Limonium callianthum, also known as sea lavender or dwarf statice, is not well-documented in terms of its medicinal uses. However, its plant family, Plumbaginaceae, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Some sources suggest that the plant may have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which could be beneficial for wound healing and skin issues. Further research is needed to confirm the potential medicinal uses of Limonium callianthum. The plant is primarily valued for its ornamental and decorative properties in gardens and floral arrangements.
taxonomy: Limonium callianthum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Plumbaginaceae. This plant is native to the Mediterranean region and is known for its bright purple flowers. The classification of Limonium callianthum is further defined by its binomial nomenclature, which is a system of naming species used in biology. Limonium callianthum is a part of the genus Limonium, which includes over 100 species of coastal plants. As a member of the Plumbaginaceae family, Limonium callianthum shares characteristics with other plants that have similar growth habits and flower structures.

It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and respiratory problems. constituents: Limonium callianthum, commonly known as the sea lavender, contains various active constituents that contribute to its medicinal properties. These constituents include flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Additionally, the plant is rich in phenolic acids, including ferulic and sinapic acid, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. intro: Limonium callianthum, also known as Sea Lavender, is a perennial plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its fragrant, blue-purple flowers and succulent leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in soothing skin irritations, reducing fever, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Limonium callianthum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Limonium callianthum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, relieve respiratory issues, and support immune function.
